description: Trx_tag — E. coli thioredoxin-1 (trxA, 109 aa) used as a N-terminal fusion partner to promote solubility of recombinant proteins in the bacterial cytoplasm.

label: Trx_tag
note: E. coli thioredoxin-1 (trxA, UniProt P0AA25), 109 aa. Widely used as a N-terminal fusion partner to enhance solubility of recombinant proteins in the E. coli cytoplasm. Thermostable (Tm ~85 °C). Released from the periplasm-adjacent fraction by osmotic shock.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:P0AA25 (Thioredoxin 1, Escherichia coli (strain K12); Swiss-Prot reviewed, SV=2), 109 aa, 100% identity over full length, independently fetched and compared

functional_claims.label: Trx_tag, fused N-terminally to a passenger protein, substantially reduces inclusion body formation and increases yields of soluble recombinant protein in the E. coli cytoplasm.

source.quote: a thioredoxin gene fusion expression system that circumvents inclusion body formation in the E. coli cytoplasm

functional_claims.label: Thioredoxin-1 is exceptionally thermostable (Tm ~85 °C), enabling a heat-treatment step (65–80 °C) to selectively denature most co-purifying E. coli host proteins while the Trx fusion remains soluble, increasing purity.

source.quote: heat treatment of thioredoxin fusions increases the purity of alpha-helical transmembrane protein constructs

functional_claims.label: Thioredoxin-1 accumulates in the osmotic-shock fraction when expressed in E. coli, allowing selective release of Trx-fusion proteins from the periplasm-adjacent compartment by osmotic shock without full cell disruption.

source.quote: thioredoxin accumulates in the osmotic shock fraction

functional_claims.label: The WCGPCK active site (residues 32–37) contains the Cys33/Cys36 redox-active disulfide; this site enables Trx to reduce protein disulfide bonds as part of the thioredoxin/thioredoxin-reductase electron-relay system.

source.quote: The CXXC motif: crystal structure of an active-site variant of Escherichia coli thioredoxin

functional_claims.label: Trx_tag lacks an intrinsic affinity handle; purification of Trx-fusion proteins typically requires a co-expressed His-tag or other affinity tag for immobilized metal affinity chromatography.

source.quote: Trx lacks intrinsic affinity handle (requires His-tag co-expression)

functional_claims.label: Trx_tag is primarily validated for solubility enhancement in Escherichia coli; use in other bacterial and eukaryotic expression systems has been reported but is less standardized.

source.quote: Thioredoxin as a fusion partner for production of soluble recombinant proteins in Escherichia coli

functional_claims.label: Trx_tag can be removed from the fusion protein by site-specific proteases (thrombin or enterokinase recognition sites are commonly inserted between Trx and the passenger); downstream processing uses the retained His-tag for subtractive IMAC.

value.removal_strategy: protease cleavage followed by subtractive IMAC

source.quote: Expression and purification of thioredoxin fusion proteins

uniprot_features.label: Site: Deprotonates C-terminal active site Cys
Site: Contributes to redox potential value
Site: Contributes to redox potential value
